hotel_name: Khách sạn Sofitel Legend Metropole Hà Nội (Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi Hotel)

reviews.title: Most Inflexible, Uncommercial and Unhelpful
reviews.text: For various reasons my visa was incomplete and I was turned away at the airport and therefore unable to make the trip. My PA attempted to call hotel to cancel booking but without success. Attempts made for subsequent credit as still intention to go to Hanoi on business but for longer period but hotel rfusing to give credit though they are aware that I would be spending more nights and money there.
reviews.positives: Not Applicable

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: location is great, beauitful grounds and the general staff that is inovled with cleanliness and service outside of the front desk managers were all amazing
reviews.negatives: the front desk treated me and my daughter disrespectfully - we had a bank blockage issue arriving into the country and had been very sick on the plane. We were not received with graciousness that a hotel of this stature should offer. Very disappointing. The general manager of the hotel apologized which was appreciated, but I hope this offers a learning for that particular part of their staff towards future guests - please do not act robotically in a hospitality role.

reviews.title: too expensive for its quality.
reviews.text: room was almost 300 dalloars per night, but it is so small. breakfast is like battlefield to get your food. so crowded. not wanna go there anymore.
reviews.positives: good location.

reviews.title: Don't book the Opera wing!
reviews.positives: Whatever you do, spend the extra money and stay on the Heritage side...it's like another planet! I had stayed there for 3 nights before taking an overnight cruise on  Halong Bay. When I tried booking the same  wing for my return, it was unavailable, so I had no option but to stay in the Opera wing. Exorbitant prices for a cramped twin room, (even the bathroom felt like a was in a hostel, it was so small, with a raised side shower/tub....SO out of date!), the staff checking you in acted as though they were doing you a favor, the key to my room didn't work and no one ever appeared with one that did! Fortunately, I was only interested in sleeping and re-grouping before heading to Danang airport, but at these prices, it would have been better to have stayed at an airport hotel!

reviews.title: Massively Overpriced
reviews.text:
Charging almost $400 for 1 night in a room around 32m square. Tried this hotel based purely on never having stopped in a a sofitel before.

The room was small, dated, and lacking in facilities. No iron, shower was tatty and old,bathroom was very average.  Staff are good and helpful but feel false. No way is this hotel worth the price.  Hotel feels claustrophobic and not aired very well. Most likely due to low ceilings 

Only upside is the breakfast which is great.

Overall in my opinion its massively overpriced and runs purely on its name and location. 

After 1 night we moved to hotel Melia which was not to far away. 

Will leave a review separately for melia as i dont want to make it look like its a biased review bigging up the melia whilst trying to belittle sofeitel
reviews.response:
Dear Lee,

We would like to thank you very much for sharing your feedback further to your stay at the Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi.

We look forward for your next visit to Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi and create more memorable experiences with you.

Sincerely,

Linh Chi NGO (Ms.)
Director of Quality and Guest Experience
Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi

reviews.title: Good
reviews.positives: Everything

reviews.title: Great location but that's about it
reviews.text:
The hotel is a lovely historical property and we booked a room in the club category with lounge access and a ' butler' however, the butler service was horrible from the word go. 

The first day our personal butler 'forgot' to carry through our bookings and as a result caused a lot if confusion and we ended up wasting half a day. The next day, despite getting an apology from the customer service director, the butler service did not improve. The butler staff were inefficient, arrogant and not at all helpful. In contrast the main concierge desk on the lobby level were far more helpful. Every requests evasion we made through the bunkers were messed up, restaurant bookings were forgotten, made at 7.30, instead of 9.30, water puppet theater bookings weren't prompt enough etc etc. we realized the uselessness of them soon, and started getting most of our bookings through the concierge at the lobby level. These club category rooms are not worth it, as in they build in the price of that extra butler service, which is hilarious now in hindsight!

A word of caution, this hotel is not for you if you are traveling with children staying in your room,nas the bathrooms have no privacy.

In a word,dissapointing,we will not go back nor recommend it.

reviews.title: Poor eggs, rude management
reviews.text: A disappointment for such an apparently legendary hotel. Whilst staff seemed friendly enough, there was a lack of the degree of efficiency one would expect from a five-star hotel. When a complaint was made about the overcooked rubbery eggs over easy served by room service, they delivered another two times, worse each time. The final time they were even burnt on the top! A French "manager"  appeared the third time and was particularly rude (not provoked), shouting that the eggs over easy had been cooked properly when the ones he saw were much worse than even the first two lots. So much for customer relations. The feeling was that the Vietnamese staff are cowed by the French management. Not a comfortable stay and we were glad to leave.
reviews.positives: Clean
reviews.negatives: Unable to cook eggs over easy properly, rude management (food and beverages?)

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: The location and the hotel is the best I stayed in Hanoi
reviews.negatives: My room was on the first floor and next to the butler room, noisy in the early mornings

reviews.title: No value for money
reviews.text: The history and colonial grandeur is what one gets at this hotel. Staff goes to great lengths to make you feel served, to the point that the waitress over breakfast keeps reading your ipad newspaper over your shoulder for more than a minute. The quality of the room is so-so, with mattresses and pillows well below expectations, and a spectacular view on all the pipings and airconditioning systems that the hotel could muster, in spite of the not so low booking class (at all). The spa is really good, especially the massage when you fly in totally jet lagged and don't feel like venturing out in town. That is good for the first day only, as a few steps outside of the hotel, one can find everything imaginable at a fraction of the price. In conclusion, I am glad to have stayed here because it is a heritage site which it would be hard to do without, but the overall satisfaction ratio is on the disappointing side when value for money is considered. Excellent breakfast buffet. On the very, very down side, when the breakfast patio was full, they let me wander in and find out myself there was no space, in the presence of an expatriate manager idly looking the other way, which is inexcusable.
reviews.positives: Colonial site

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: location

reviews.title: Functional
reviews.text:
Rooms are decorated nicely. The public areas look drab, with a rabbit warren of corridors that feel more confusing than charming. It feels more like a government administrative building than a relaxing hotel. 
Reception staff were unsmiling, robotic — and frankly, rude. No intuition for dealing with people outside the box. We didn’t feel welcomed at all. Just processed.
The staff also seemed culturally unaware. It felt like they expect a certain kind of guest — and anyone outside of that profile..

Regards food, the pesto pasta was excellent. The rest of the food we tried was average at best. Pool side fruit juices watery.

I later discovered through other reviews that the hotel offers tours, has a historic bunker, afternoon tea, and a happy hour. Would’ve loved to experience any of that — but no one mentioned a thing. Zero communication from the staff.
Not sure what else was going on at the hotel because I wasn’t informed or invited to anything.

All in all, ok experience. Would not return.
reviews.response:

However, it is disheartening to learn that your experience in our public areas and with our reception staff did not meet your expectations. We strive to create an inviting atmosphere for all our guests, and it is concerning to hear that you did not feel welcomed during your stay. We take guest interaction seriously and will be reviewing our training procedures to ensure our team members embodies the warmth and hospitality we are known for.

I also regret that you were not informed about the various amenities and activities we offer, including our tours and afternoon tea. Effective communication with our guests is a cornerstone of our

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: Upon arrival, the hotel kindly upgraded our rooms and offered us a very generous voucher to use in the hotel facilities. We used this voucher for airport transfer for me and my four friends, We rarely see in most hotels.
reviews.negatives:
The hotel is excellent and beautiful, with a great location. Everything was very good overall.
However, I have a few general comments that I hope can be improved:

	• The check-in and check-out were slow. Unfortunately, there was only one staff member handling this process during both our arrival and departure, which took around 15 to 25 minutes just to check in.
	
	• The next morning, the Guitar music was extremely loud and woke me up, but once I contacted the reception, they immediately turned it off.

Overall, the hotel is truly beautiful, and I highly recommend staying here.

reviews.text:
This was my third stay in this hotel in 6 yrs
I found the front staff was very clinical and barely welcomed and strait wanted credit card on numerous occasions in in appropriate times like right in the middle of meals. If I have prepaid $3000 for 4 days on two rooms - I am definitely not wanting not to pay for additional services.  Also I mentioned bathroom door was off leading to puddle of water. They said they fixed it but it was still the same Nuisance.  This for a 500$ plus room. Not acceptable!!
Will not everyone staying here again.

reviews.positives: This is a very good hotel and was once considered the best hotel in Hanoi.The hotel is elegantly decorated and has several excellent restaurants and bars. Prices are on the higher side, but the food is delicious. The entire staff is professional and welcoming.
reviews.negatives: Our key card coulodn't work in the elevator.As someone who is almost 60, it was embarrassing to be ridiculed by a twentysomething passenger in the elevator as if we don't know how to use the elevator.For a hotel of this caliber, such a mistake is outrageous.

reviews.title: A great hotel in service decay
reviews.text:
After our previous stay in 2010, which was an exceptional experience, I rated this hotel as one of the greatest in the world. 
My primary purpose for coming to Hanoi was to revisit my Metropole experience, let me say I was very disappointed. My impressions of this hotel now, is that it is still “Grand Hotel” however rather than making guests welcome , the hotel has adopted an approach that guests should feel privileged to stay at the hotel. 
From check-in we tried to upgrade our reservation to include breakfast, however there was no flexibility in this regard and were told if we wished to have breakfast we be charged the full price of $25.
The service in the Bamboo Bar was best described as poor, on at least two occasions my coffee was warm and not hot, this was because the waiter had forgotten order , now if you intend to charge world-wide premium price for coffee, at minimum the coffee should be hot.
We dined at Angelina’s it was one of the worst dining experiences I have encountered , the staff were not attentive , and were intent on sharing images on their I phone’s. On a nearby table there was lady who was demanding and rude, she in fact received silver service; maybe we should have adopted her approach. We were made to feel that we were hindrance and the focus , was on moving us on, our meals , came in quick succession we were not asked if wished to order wine and actually had draw staff’s attention to get service.
The next morning we had breakfast in Le Beaulieu, it is the only time in my lifetime that I can recall that we were presented with the check before we had completed breakfast.
Will I be recommending this hotel in the future? Yes with provisos, if you want to do business in Hanoi, then this maybe the hotel for you. If you are on vacation look elsewhere there are other  hotels that represent better service and value.
The hotel has been defined by its special character and values, this has been what has made the hotel great, however this has bee

reviews.title: No Wifi in Rooms, Pay $5 an hour for Wifi in Foyer
reviews.text:
ok, so maybe the hotel was built in 1901 but i'm living in 2011. the history and aesthetics of a building can only take you so far. i'm traveling light and relying my iphone and apps to maximise my travel experience. to add insult to injury, the local guide book with restaurants etc i received from concierge, talked about " wifi" even at your local noodle shop. 

as an australian, i found the service here contrived. staff tried to look like they cared, but they didn't. at concierge i asked whether there was a restaurant strip where i might find local food. i was told no. the next day i was given a hotel map with "vietnamese cuisine streets" clearly marked. the woman clearly understood, there wasn't a language issue and she recommended the restaurants in the hotel.

paid $51 per person for a four hour trip of the city organised by the hotel (guide plus driver). it took us to the old quarter twice, once by cyclo, once walking around. it's a five min walk from the hotel and we had already explored ourselves. but we didn't see other expected sites, and it seemed such a waste of having access to an air-conditioned car and driver for four hours. for example, didn't see cathedral or the the bridge, or even have the opera house pointed out to us.

overall the location is good, and facilities all you would expect from a hotel of this rating. i stayed in the historic wing and it was quaint. i loved the l'occitane products in the bathroom, and this is the lesson for the hotel: it's actually about the small things.

i'd probably try the hilton next time.
reviews.positives: None

We enjoyed our stay, the staff were amazing and helpful.

A must visit hotel and a must live experience.

The French restaurant food was a disappointment and for the price I would have expected a better quality.

reviews.title: luxury but not five-star customer service
reviews.text:
I have been staying in this hotel chain in several locations in the world but this one has given me the lowest rating in term of customer service. 
Flying from hcmc to haiphong for meetings and from haiphong to hanoi by car, getting to the hotel was late in the evening and very tired but my check-in was not very pleasant at all: no queen room available, strong detergent smell in the room, keycard did not work for 3 times. 
Furthermore, I have asked for a pen as forgotten mine in the meeting but I couldn't find one in the room; disappointedly, the receptionist was hesitate to give me and asked me:" dear miss, where can I find a pen if I give it to you?". What kind of question is that when I paid $700 for 2 nights staying in this hotel and asking for 1 pen I don't think it is any expensive or tackle request here. 
In addition, i would like to rent a motorbike to visit the city for a few hours before heading to the airport but the hotel apparently know only 1 bike renter so they replied to me that I have to manage by myself!
After all, requesting for a pen and renting motorbike service would be common requests from a customer but disappointed I didn't receive a five-star service from this hotel. 
Next time I visit this city either for business or pleasure, I will not stay in this hotel again. There are many other options that i could go for in the town and I hope that the experience will be better than Sofitel Metropole.
reviews.positives: comfortable bed, luxury design, quick house-keeping service

reviews.title: One of the worst experiences at checking-in
reviews.text:
We are regular visitors at the Metropole and in general have been satisfied with the establishment. However, we experienced one on the worst experiences during our recent stay. The attitude of the staffs at the check-in counter was shocking. The way they treated and responded to us were borderline insulting, one staff even made a sarcastic joke and laughed at us as he was looking away. 
To make matter worst, we left a complaint email to the hotel but received no response. We won’t be coming back there again.
reviews.response:

Your description of our staff's behavior and attitude is concerning and does not align with the high standards we strive to uphold. Please know that we take your comments seriously, and I will address this matter with our team to ensure that similar incidents do not occur in the future.

reviews.title: Beautiful classic property!
reviews.text: Such a beautiful property. Take time to explore Le Balcon, and walk around the property. Request a room in the "old wing" if you want high ceilings and character. A bit run-down, but much more romantic than the "new wing". Great location to the lake (about 5-10min walk) and the pool and pool staff very friendly. If you want to tour their bomb shelter, plan in advance b/c they only have 3 a day and you need to register. Will definitely go back!!

reviews.title: Historic hotel with just a bit too many people
reviews.text: I had stayed here a couple of times before their big renovation and I remembered it to be more charming.  Now, there is just too many people, especially in the lobby in the AM between the tour groups checking out, the tour guides and drivers milling about, it's almost like a market or a 3* tourist hotel. The 7th fl club floor staff are great and super helpful.  But the ambience in the club floor has lost its exclusivity, there are just too many people now packed in for the tea session and the cocktail hours.  The breakfast in the meeting room turned breakfast room is a mess by 9AM.  they don't bother to clean up after the people, again that feeling of 3* hotel in China after the busload of tourists have come through.  The staff are also way below 5* level.

The breakfast was the most wonderful selection of food gorgeously presented in the most lovely room.

We loved our cocktails in the bamboo bar.

I stay in Hanoi for 7 days,the weather is hot,I try to swim in the morning time but the staff told me the swimming pool was under repair n unknown for how long,if I want to swim i can go to Hilton Hotel which is across the road after walking for about 5 minutes.It is rediculous and I won't walk in wet with swimming robe on the road.The hotel without notice to stayin guest until I call up to the front desk.For this inconvenient hotel without any apology note.
The room is up to standard but the toilet flush always out of water,it should not be happen to the deluxe hotel.
Instead to stay 5 days I changed my mind after stay for 2 ngt.
reviews.positives: location is good

reviews.title: You deserve a slice of Heaven in Hanoi
reviews.positives: Gorgeous property located in a quieter area of Hanoi. The hotel is a throwback to elegance; staff are professionals through and through, comfortable rooms well stocked with high end amenities, lush floral decorations throughout and thoughtful and much appreciated personal touches that spell “welcome and enjoy.” I might add we are not usually given to booking high end hotels on vacation but we decided to splurge and treat ourselves to several days of luxury for our 30th wedding anniversary vacation. Going out and exploring a city is our focus  and on this trip, it was glorious to come back from the hustle and bustle of Hanoi and unwind on the property.
reviews.negatives: There wasn’t a thing we could mention.

reviews.title: The best stay in Hanoi

reviews.title: Needs a new General Manager
reviews.text: If like me, you like Raffles Hotel then this is the right hotel for you. However, this is Vietnam and not Singapore. The hotel gets all the big things right but what lets this hotel down and the Sofitel brand is the small things. They might be small but they build up. For example, the coffee machine has such a short lead that the only place it can go is in front of the TV. The bed has no bolsters, the toilet doesn’t have a butt gun and newspapers are not delivered to your room but left in a pile at the top of the stairs. I was pleased to be told that the room had a smart TV but it has not been properly set up. In other hotels, I’ve stayed at there is a welcome screen with the date and time and a weather forecast, then there are the usual options to chose from. I asked the young lady who showed me to the room how I could get screen sharing. She played with the remote and clearly had no idea what she was doing so I asked if the hotel had an IT specialist. He was called for and he too had no idea. Tried to tell me as my tab was Sony and the TV Samsung it wasn’t possible! However, I had got him to change the language to English and was then able to show them how to do it. I went to the swimming pool there was no one swimming but many of the loungers had hats, books or glasses left on them so there were none spare for me and my partner. Although we were there for more than an hour the loungers remained unoccupied. There should be a notice banning this practice and making it clear that if the loungers are not used for a period of half an hour, personal belongings will be removed and the loungers made available to other guests. Finally, at check out the receptionists do not smile or make eye contact, as the queue built up it was one of the door attendants saw the delay and went and got someone from the back office to help clear the backlog.  A tall balding westerner in a suit had wandered over and worked on a computer behind the counter and then wandered off, clearly bored

reviews.title: Decadance in the middle of Hanoi
reviews.text:
The Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi Hotel is conveniently located just off one of the corners of the lake in the centre.  Upon entering either lobby (there are two), there is a sense of calm and tranquility.  The hotel interior is grand and is beautifully decorated and furnished.  The rooms are similarly opulent and spacious.  The bed is very comfortable, the decor is grand and the bathroom is large and well lit.  There is also a turn down service.

The hotel staff were generally helpful.  I had a few issues on check-in because we arrived early and the room was not available, but as soon as it was the staff came to find me to escort me to the room.

The hotel breakfast buffet was good, but certainly not the best that I have experienced and I would say that I expected more from a 5 star hotel.  The selection was fair and the quality was okay.

Overall, a well located and quality hotel in Hanoi, but I expect there are other hotels that can compete with the Metropole in many aspects and cost a fraction of the price.
reviews.positives: Opulent rooms, tranquil interior, nice pool area

reviews.title: Proper five star hotel

reviews.title: An exceptional stay in a quiet oasis in the city
reviews.positives:
Exceptional experience! It was our wedding anniversary and the staff did their best for us during our stay to celebrate this day - like giving us flowers, box of heart shaped chocolate and a very cute hand written card. Also our room was upgraded to a better one, which was also very nice. 
The breakfast was amazing - lots of food to choose from and also the place where it is served is very beautiful. 
The room was always super clean, the staff attentive and friendly. So definitely can recommend for anyone coming to Hanoi to enjoy some peace and quiet in the bustling town. 
Also it was interesting to participate in the tour of the hotel and to see the history behind this building. 
Would love to come back here again! Definitely a great value and a real five star experience.
reviews.negatives:
The room was a little bit too small, basically just bed and a small table and chair. But since it’s a historic building in the city center it’s understandable. 
Also our windows were overlooking the street and it could get a little noisy sometimes. For example there were some celebrations on the street and the music could be heard until 11pm or so, and early in the morning (6-7am) we could hear the music from the small park nearby where they do tai chi. 
So maybe if you want a quiet night it’s better to ask for the inner garden view. 
However these small things didn’t influence our overall experience and all in all it was an amazing and memorable stay.

reviews.text: The hotel itself oozes beauty & indulgence, & the staff are extremely well trained to ensure you feel special as their guest. reviews.title: clean, nice, expensive - - is it really worth it?
reviews.text:
the hotel is nice, there is some history here. we stayed in the old wing --- so called luxury room. it wasnt really luxury, just old and historical. the price for this room is expensive, not really worth it when comparing to other 5 and 4 star hotels. as usual all internal facility extras are about 4-5 times more expensive than in the surrounding city like food, massage, etc. the hotel service is very good. they keep to the french nature with most speaking in french. most of the rooms belongs to an embassy in the past, we stayed in the room that used to be the german embassy. we had a sneak peek at the australian embassy room and that was nicer. the ppol is average, the chocolate buffet is an experience but not worth it. they claim to have the best french restaurant in hanoi, i agree with that comment, pretty hard to find good western food in the city. they have proper french chefs but expect to pay $$$.

all i have to say is, visit it if you need to, otherwise probably get a more luxury room at a nearby 4 or 5 star hotel for the same $$$ and bigger. avoid the hilton, its terrible.

reviews.title: Hôtel de légende dans un quartier stratégique pour visiter Hanoï. Bémol: la clientèle d'Affaire.
reviews.positives: L'hôtel Métropole est une légende dans l'histoire de Hanoï et fait partie des palaces mythiques dans le monde. Il est très bien situé dans le quartier des ambassades, à une centaine de mètres du lac Hoan Kiem et pas très loin. du vieux quartier d'Hanoï.
reviews.negatives: Cet hotel est très fréquenté par une clientèle d'affaires internationales (dont beaucoup de Français- point important si vous avez envie d'être dépaysé), si bien que votre cocktail en amoureux le soir au bord de la piscine  est accompagné de rires bruyants  de Businessmen  alcoolisés et,  le matin, au petit déjeuner, vous êtes entourés de personnes sur PC en visioconférence... D'autre part, si vous prenez le vol du soir,  le coût du check-out tardif à 18h est très cher (la moitié du prix de la chambre pour 6 heures d'occupation supplémentaires). Pour le Vietnam, c'est très cher et au final, si ce n'est pas votre entreprise qui paye le séjour (établissement Accor), peut-être s'offrir un palace plus "dans son jus" vers le lac et aller au Metropole pour boire un cocktail!

reviews.title: nice hotel, not for the budget traveller
reviews.text: We stayed there to enjoy the atmosphere of an old colonial building. In that aspect, we were treated well as the building is beautiful and great care is taken to enhance the romantic and historical value of the hotel.............The room we had in the Historical Wing was very comfortable though quite smaller in comparison with the other hotels we stayed in (when we only payed around 30US$ for them), with a nice wooden floor and stylish furniture but the view from the window was mediocre (a wall or something)............. The pool is nice and the staff thoroughly efficient (the lady at the concierge whom we asked to book our restaurant was efficient and the man who got our taxi to the airport was fast) but when we realized after shower that they had forgotten to supply us with towels in our bathroom, it took two phone calls and a good 15mn-20mn to get them despite the fact that we asked them to supply them fast...............The continental breakfast (which was not included in the 200+ USD we payed) is wonderful, probably one of the best breakfast we've ever had in any hotel, with an exquisite selection of fruit, pastries, dairies and so on but at a whopping 25US$ per head...............Wi-fi is at 5USD/hour which is unbelievable considering you can catch wi-fi almost everywhere in this country.................So, in comparison with the value you can get for 30USD in other hotels in Vietnam, you can not help but think that it's overall way too expensive for what it is, but if you have the means, this is a beautiful hotel to stay at location-wise and comfort-wise.

reviews.title: Excellent Hotel, shame about the spa.
reviews.text:
Had a wonderful stay at the old colonial hotel in their new wing. I had stayed at the hotel before, years ago when the hotel was looking a bit tired - but they have totally rejuvenated the hotel. 
The only fault we found with the hotel was the spa. The spa looks great, but is very expensive. It is the worst massage I have ever had in the whole of asia! Firstly, we booked 60 mins. at 2.15 we were asked to sign in, fill in form etc. then we taken up stairs for tea, then after tea to the room for the massage, where we disrobed, had the massage, got dressed, then downstairs for more tea and then to reception to sign the bill - at which point I noticed it was only 3.10!!! The massage must have only been 30mins. Total rip off!
I also booked a swedish massage and asked for medium firm (they have boxes to tick on the form) and requested they concentrate on my shoulders and upper back. The massage "therapist" (and I use the term loosely) completely ignored the sheet and did her own thing - which was just slightly more than being stroked!
At the desk we were asked how the massage was - and I felt compelled to be honest. I was then told by the spa manager that I should have picked a different massage if I wanted it firmer?! Apparently the customer is not always right in the spa!
In conclusion, lovely hotel, avoid the spa - unless you like a very expensive tickle!

reviews.title: Old style charm with nice touches
reviews.text:
we enjoyed our stay at the metropole as it was like an oasis for us. after walking around the old quarter and french quarter, it was great to come back for a swim in the pool and not hear the beeping of horns (it was out first stop in vietnam- we are now used to the beep beep noise!)

the hotel rooms are quite nice- we stayed in the old wing which was furnished with lots of teak. i found it a little amusing that you could buy anything in the room (e.g. clock...) ah well, why not? the bed was very hard, which i did not like so much, but my husband loved. the bathroom was large and had a separate bath and shower with l'occitane products. we read the club rooms had hermes products... 

we ate by the pool on one day and was very surprised to find the prices so high- in some cases much much higher than what we would pay at home. at the end of the day, you are staying in an expensive hotel so i would expect to pay a premium. serves us right for being lazy!!!

i think the best part about the hotel is the small touches that make the stay memorable, like serving free ice cream or little plates of fruit at various times of the day. the location is great- not to far to the old town and a couple of minutes walk to the lake. there are patisseries nearby and a good selection of restaurants. free wifi too... if you can afford to stay here, i would definitely recommend it.

reviews.title: Historic charm
reviews.text: Everything perfect and highly recommended with the exception of the restaurant. Tasteless dry spring rolls. Chicken was not eatible (too chewy and rubber like). Seems like the attention goes  only into presentation and not food quality. Unexpected disappointment and by far the worst dinner during a 5-day stay in Hanoi.
reviews.response:
Dear Christoph,

Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback. We sincerely appreciate your kind words about the historic charm of our property and are glad to hear that you found everything else to be perfect and highly recommendable.

However, we are truly sorry to learn about your disappointing experience with our restaurant. Please accept our sincere apologies for the tasteless and dry spring rolls, as well as the chewy, rubber-like chicken. This is certainly not up to the standards we aim to uphold, and we regret that your dinner did not meet your expectations.

Please rest assured, we will address this issue with our culinary team to improve both the taste and texture of our dishes. Our goal is to offer a memorable dining experience that complements the overall quality of our service and ambiance.

We deeply regret that this was the worst dinner of your 5-day stay in Hanoi and that it caused an unexpected disappointment. We would love the opportunity to make this

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel
reviews.text:
This hotel is absolutely beautiful and is in a prime location. Further it’s such a cozy atmosphere and historical.  I was very impressed! 
Most of the staff did an amazing job. The staff at bamboo bar gave excellent service and made sure we felt at home and the same goes for the doormen. They were there to help before you even knew you needed help and with a big smile. 

The staff at the front desk however could deliver a little better service.  At check out it just felt a bit like we were just the next in line. It was minimum interaction and mostly just about payment. I ended up being the one wishing her a good day. A little sad to leave on such a note. Luckily again the doormen was amazing and sent us of in a good way. 

The room was beautiful but unfortunately felt stuffy and humid which I don’t think is acceptable for a hotel this standard. Also drain in bathroom tent to not work properly. 

Food was absolutely amazing and tasty! Room service was just on point as well!
reviews.response:
Dear Maria,

Thank you very much for taking the time to share your feedback regarding your recent stay at Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi. We are delighted to hear that you found our hotel beautiful and appreciated its prime location and cozy, historical atmosphere. It is always our aim to create a memorable experience for our guests, and we are thrilled that many aspects of your stay met your expectations.

We are particularly pleased to learn that our team at the Bamboo Bar and our doormen made a positive impact on your stay with their attentive service and warm smiles. Such commendations are invaluable to us, as they reflect our commitment to excellence in hospitality. However, we sincerely regret that the service you experienced at our front desk did not align with the high standards we strive to maintain. We value your feedback and will ensure that it is addressed with our team, as we continually seek to improve our guest interactions.

Furthermore, we apologize for the discomfort

reviews.title: Vintage hotel with great service and rooms
reviews.text:
we picked this hotel as it was a historic hotel and it also has the reputation of best hotel in vietnam. we were not disappointed. we stayed at the historical wing rather than the opera wing although we initially made a reservation for the opera wing. after visiting both wings, we decided on upgrading to the historic wing as the opera wing looked like any other modern hotel. the rooms in the historical wing was really beautiful and the bed was extremely soft and comfortable. you can also request for different pillows to suit your sleepin posture.

location wise, sofitel metropole is about a 15 min walk to hoan kiem lake. there are also nice bakeries around this area. the luxury brands like lv, hermes, gucci are also located in this area if you are thinking of getting any luxury goods.

however, i was a little disappointed when i was checking out as the reception did not have enough usd to offer me back my change. i had paid for my expenses using cash and paid in usd. i was expecting change of about 30 usd but the reception only had  usd as change for me and they offered to pay me the remainder in vnd. i explained that i do not need vnd as i am leaving vietnam, but they said that they really did not have anymore usd. this really disappointed me as a hotel like sofitel did not even have 30 usd for change and it was only 11am in the morning on a working day.
reviews.positives: Historic hotel, nice decor

reviews.title: Hors du temps
reviews.positives: La beauté du bâtiment et la gentillesse du personnel

reviews.title: Classic luxury hotel
reviews.text:
I would recommend this hotel to first-time visitors to Hanoi because it is centrally located and extremely comfortable. Food and drinks are on the high side, but there are other restaurants nearby.
The staff are courteous and helpful. Always ask the concierge to call a taxi and to give you a card with the address of the hotel for your return journey. Otherwise the more cunning drivers will take you to the other Sofitel hotel which is much further away. Also check the taxi meter: A 300 000 Dong reading does not equate to a 3 million Dong (as one driver tried to convince me). When in doubt seek assistance from your hotel or restaurant.
reviews.positives: Historic hotel offering all the comfort and luxury you would expect

reviews.title: Hôtel très bien place et calme
reviews.positives: Le service et la beauté du lieu

reviews.title: historical grandeur and prices to match
reviews.text: liked the hotel, service was quick, though initially put in a relatively small room for our two adults and two kids. However, some discussion with the manager got us a larger room in the same price category (the advantage ? of these older hotels: not all rooms are identical). Once that was sorted we enjoyed the room. The pool is a bit short for laps, but great for kids, both those learning to swim as well as those already swimming. Not so ideal for small kids who cannot swim at all - the wading area is quite small. Restaurants have great food, but prices as you would expect at the Sofitel. 10-15 minute walk to the Old Quarter, but straight forward.
reviews.positives: Charming history, personality

reviews.title: Lovely hotel with lots of character and nostalgia
reviews.text: My boyfriend and I stayed in Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi Hotel for a total of 4 days and loved it. While it's certainly not a cheap hotel, it's one where you get your money's worth. The service and staff were impeccable and everything from the live jazz every evening in the hotel bar to the L'Occitane-branded toiletries made our stay pampered and looked-after. There were chocolate every night and a paper in the morning. We stayed in both the old and the new wings and I would recommend those who like those who like a huge bathtub to stay in the old wing as it was the biggest and nicest hotel bathtub I've ever been in!
reviews.positives: beautiful and old hostorical building, great spa, very romantic

Merci à Madama Quoi pour son aide précieuse.
Nous reviendrons très vite !

reviews.title: A wonderful hotel in grear location
reviews.text:
A really wonderful hotel, although there are some glitches that need to be addressed before you could describe the Metropole as one of the best in Asia. Allowing smoking in the restaurants was a real issue for us (although generally the case across Vietnam). Some rooms (notably 116 in our experience) can be a little noisey, but not too bad for a City hotel.

Staff were very well trained and attentive, with some true stand-outs. Public areas and rooms were spacious and beautifully furnished. Good size bedrooms and bathrooms. Hotel is really interested in the quality of what they provide their customers including the tours that they sell via the hotel.
reviews.positives: Great atmosphere; beautiful building;superb location; some excellent staff

Wenn man mit booking bucht, ist man besonders angeschmiert. Das mag kein Hotel.Da sind die schlechtesten Zimmer vor programmiert, aber auch die sind bei Sofitel nicht so schlecht!
reviews.negatives: Da ich mit booking gebucht hatte ..... wird man sehr bescheiden empfangen, auf den Zimmern nicht mal ein Easser oder gar ein kleines Schreiben,,das man als wiederkehrender Gast in allen anderen Hotels erwartet.

Location is very convenient for sight-seeing and nightlife... Price was steep for Hanoi, especially versus perfectly adequate alternatives, but you do still get your money's worth.

reviews.text: This is absolutely Hanoi's best hotel and it is definately worth the extra cost for the stay, although I felt it used to be better in the past it is still great, the sunday brunch is also good. The rooms in the historical wing are quite small but we did not mind that much as the over all stay was great. The new bar where in the corner where metpub used to be located was nice.

reviews.text: This is the nicest hotel in Hanoi and I really enjoyed the stay, with excellent staffs that everyone of them was train to help any of your needs. Rooms are very comfortable and spacious, beautifully decorated, they even have butler service on the club floor which I highly recommend, complimentary breakfast, afternoon tea and cocktails, although they can make it perfect by giving out full drinks instead of half portion on the cocktails, I understand that it is free and the hotel is trying to control the consumption on the liquors, but when you decided to give out it is better to give 100%.
reviews.positives: Excellent staffs, beautiful rooms, nice location.

reviews.title: Fantastique!
reviews.positives:
Kolonial Style , historisches Gebäude. Gute Sicherheit weil President Macron auch im Haus Gast war! :))
Hervorragendes Essen im Restaurant Beaulieu mit ein super nette Kellner der nach 30 Sekonden bemerkt ich bin Links Händler und unbemerkt das Besteck umgewechselt hat ,ich war beeindruckt. der Lounge beim Pool ist auch toll. Nettes personal überall. Schöne ruhige Suiten Richtung Innenhof mit tollen Weitblick im oberen Etagen.
Metropole Club Lounge (executive floor), gutes Frühstück und ruhig.
Ich bekam beim Abreise ein herrliche Tee in schönen Kanister und ein grossen Topf Vietnamesischen Honing ,wir kämpfen jetzt um den letzten Tropfen so Fein! Ein Grund nochmal hin zu fliegen ;)

reviews.title: Nice hotel in central Hanoi
reviews.text:
We had a great couple of days in Hanoi.  Concierge gave a great recommendation for the Ly Club restaurant (although I think the soft shell crab was a bit dodgy) - the hotel itself was fabulous and gives you a good feel for colonial hanoi.


Do not believe Lonely planet's "seafood street" blurb - we drove along the whole street for several kilometers and found nothing - luckily we were happy to go to a VERY local vietnamese restaurant on the lake - very good prawns, lucky I'd made it to chapter 3 of Teach yourself vietnamese!
reviews.positives: Location, staff, atmosphere

reviews.positives: Ein sehr schöner Ort und das mitten im Herz der Großstadt. Der Innenhof, der Pool, die Ruhe. Wunderbar!

reviews.title: Staying at the Metroploe
reviews.text: It is a vrey pleasant and traditional hotel, and the rooms are charming and more individual than other typical business hotels. I like the shops they have, although expensive. It is a cozy atmosphere, but the food and drink tends to be on the expensive side. I think internet should be free and rather rise the hotel cost, as it is annoying to pay extra for internet nowadays. The walk to the opera wing is a bit long.
reviews.positives: Charming hotel, old colonial style. Good facilities ad great location. Godd food.
reviews.negatives: Slow service in bar restauant last day! Expensive wine, and very little in each glass (paid by glass).

reviews.title: Exceptional

Es war ein sehr angenehmer Aufenthalt im Hotel Metropole und ich freue mich bereits jetzt auf den nächsten Besuch. 
Das gesamte Personal war durchgehend freundlich und zuvorkommend. Besonders Claire hat mit ihrer natürlichen Herzlichkeit und ihrem außergewöhnlichen Engagement einen bleibenden Eindruck hinterlassen.

reviews.title: Good, but can be better
reviews.text:
As the best hotel in Hanoi, overall was good, but one thing could be improved.
Both husband and I got sore throat maybe because bedclothes were bit dusty. We stayed some nights, and  we couldn't stop coughing every night, especially in bed. Furthermore, corners of floor and shelves were covered with dust.

reviews.positives: Wir hatten ein DZ für 1,5 Tage vor unserem Heimflug gebucht. Das Hotel ist ein Traum. Geschmackvoll eingerichtet, liebevoll gestalteter Garten, weitläufig. Im Gardenrestaurant zu frühstücken bleibt unvergesslich. Das Personal ist sehr freundlich und hilfsbereit. Zurzeit begleitet einen etwas Baulärm von nebenan und aus dem alten Trakt. Aber es hat den Genuss nicht eingeschränkt. Dank einer Führung, die das Haus anbietet, kann man in die besondere Geschichte und Bedeutung des Hotels eintauchen. Vor allem der freigelegte Bunker hat uns sehr beeindruckt. Die Lage des Hotels im französischen Viertel ist bestens. Zum Hoan Kiem See sind es nur wenige Minuten. Wir würden jederzeit wieder im Metropole logieren.

reviews.title: 期待どおりの素敵なホテル
reviews.positives:
とても居心地の良いお部屋でした。
スタッフの方々も親切で英語がいまいちの私たちにも感じよく丁寧に接してくださいました。

予約はオペラウイングでしたが、ヘリテージウイングに空きがあったようでそちらに通していただきました。
古くて趣きのあるもの好きの私たちには嬉しい限りでした。

部屋に入り、しばらくするとお部屋係の方が不備な点はないかと聞きにみえて、小箱を渡してくださいました。
中にはマカロン、2泊目は蜂蜜の小瓶でした。ちょっと嬉しいプレゼントです。

スーツケースのキーの調子が悪く開かなくなってしまったのでフロントに相談しに行ったら、その場に居合わせたスタッフの方々が代わるがわる手を尽くしてくださり、開けることが出来ました。たいへんお世話になりました。

フレンチのディナーをいただきました。
お料理に合わせたワインが付くコースで、お料理はひと皿ごとに使っている蜂蜜の説明がなされ、きれいな花が描かれたカードをテーブルの小さなイーゼルに立てて置かれました。その時限定のコースだったのでしょうか、楽しい演出だと思いました。

またハノイを訪れることがあったら、是非もう一度泊まりたいホテルです。
reviews.negatives: 部屋の照明のスイッチがわかりにくかったです。何度かぱちぱちしてやっと理解しました。

reviews.title: Pampered stay in a historical luxury icon
reviews.text: Billed as an iconic Asia establishment, the Metropole doesn't disappoint. The reception procedure was polished to the highest French-accented degree, and the historical wing room was high-ceilinged and evocative of the period. In a world of mega-structures, the hotel has maintained a charming, human-scale aspect whilst accommodating modern needs. The breakfast buffet, the Spices restaurant and the Bamboo Bar under lazily revolving ceiling fans are all recommended. The fitness room is very well equipped. A nightcap at the outdoor La Terrasse will be accompanied by the noise and smog of the un-ending scooter traffic, yet this perhaps only adds to the appeal. Recommended as a luxury stay.
reviews.positives: History, finesse, ambience

the only issue i had was with their spa services.  i ended up waiting at the reception for 40 minutes for seemingly no reason though i had a reservation made in advance.  also, the hands of the masseuse were rough on my skin and it was a little painful.  

but after i sent them feedback on their services, they sent me a very sincere greeting email.  i think it is worth going back there if i had a chance!
